_comment: Data-driven intent classification rules for EU/ICAO phraseology. Loaded by src/atc/intent_rules.cpp. Schema: see intent_rules.hpp. Order of `rules` is priority order — first match wins. `normalize` patches Whisper transcription quirks before matching. `adjustments` apply post-match overrides (intent reassignment + confidence mutation) in array order.

normalize._note: Whisper drops the first 'r' in 'departure' (observed LSZB log 2026-06-07).
Voxtral drops the trailing 'e' from 'mode' in transponder readbacks (e.g. 'Mode Charlie' → 'Mod Charlie').
Voxtral mishears 'Chambery' as 'Chamber' or 'Chambry'. Normalise to correct city name.
Voxtral drops the trailing 'y' from Chambery.
Voxtral mishears 'tower' as 'our' in frequency-change readbacks (e.g. 'contact our 118.200').
Voxtral mishears 'climbing to' as 'climb in to' (drops 'ing', adds 'in').
Voxtral mishears 'climbing to' as 'climb with' (drops 'ing', adds 'with').

rules._note: Profanity / insults toward ATC. Highest priority so tone is caught even when other intent keywords are present.
Pilot-issued correction phraseology (rejecting ATC's interpretation). 'negative contact' / 'no contact' are EU traffic-advisory replies and must NOT be claimed here.
IFR clearance request to Delivery. Must come before REQUEST_TAXI / INITIAL_CALL_GROUND to prevent 'request taxi IFR' from being swallowed by the taxi rule.
Engine startup request — after IFR clearance readback, before taxi.

Pilot-initiated frequency change announcement (not a request, not ATC's own 'approved' readback). Either an unconditional sign-off phrase, OR a 'frequency change' phrase that is NOT a request and NOT ATC's approval ack.
Traffic-advisory replies must come BEFORE SELF_ANNOUNCE so 'traffic in sight' is not claimed by the CTAF announce rule.

IFR holding-point position report without explicit readiness. Must come BEFORE READBACK — bare 'holding short'/'holding point' would otherwise match READBACK's compound rules. 'none' guards prevent collision with READY_FOR_DEPARTURE (ready), REQUEST_TAXI (taxi), and READBACK ack words (wilco/roger/qnh/via).

Readback covers wilco/roger, frequency-change ack, sign-off phrases, control-zone clearances, taxi/clearance instruction echoes, takeoff/landing readbacks, VFR cross-country approvals, and transmissions ending in a runway-number token. 'holding point'/'hold short' only match when paired with a readback-context word (qnh/via/information) to avoid shadowing REPORT_HOLDING_SHORT. Explicit 'request' phrases (request taxi, request startup, request clearance) are excluded so a runway-token suffix does not swallow a service request. 'when ready' catches the frequency-handoff readback pattern ('Ground on 121.735, when ready') even when Voxtral/Whisper drops the word 'contact'. The compound none guard exempts 'contact'-prefixed phrases so 'contact ground on 121.735 when ready for departure' still fires READBACK even if 'departure' is present.
VFR cross-country departure: ready-for-departure plus a course/direction marker.
Parking/apron/etc. as the destination ('taxi to parking') — NOT pilot's location ('on parking, request taxi').

'clear of runway' / 'vacated runway' but NOT 'cleared for takeoff runway'. Whisper drops the second 'f' on 'take off' → 'take of', so guard against 'for take' (catches both spellings). 'left runway 14' counts as vacated, but 'left downwind' / 'left base' does not.
Plain 'request taxi' but exclude frequency-change readbacks and taxi-instruction readbacks (which mention 'taxi' but are echoes, not requests).
Position-Reports. 'report' (Tower-imperative) excluded so the pilot's readback of 'report downwind/final' is not misclassified as a position report.
Inbound/landing request, but only when no facility keyword (Tower/Approach) is present — those route to INITIAL_CALL_INBOUND/APPROACH instead.
US-specific VFR advisory service.
Calls to Approach Control, Departure Control, or Radar. Negative guards reject flight-phase nouns ('final approach', 'after departure') and readback phrases that happen to contain the facility word.
IFR en-route descent request. Pilot asks Centre to descend. Guards against readbacks ('descend FL110') and initial calls containing 'descend'.

En-route initial call to Area Control / UIR Centre (facility word: 'control' or 'centre'/'center'). Fires before INITIAL_CALL_GROUND so 'Brest Control' doesn't accidentally match ground. Negative guards catch readbacks of handoff instructions ('contact Control on 119.755').

'contact ground' and 'when ready' indicate the pilot is reading back a frequency-handoff instruction ('contact Ground on 121.735 when ready'), not making an actual initial call to Ground. Voxtral sometimes drops 'contact', leaving 'Ground on 121.735 when ready' — 'when ready' guards that case.
Tower call with inbound/landing intent — beats INITIAL_CALL_TOWER below.

Pure initial contact only. If the transmission already carries a clear follow-up token (ready / request taxi/landing/touch / downwind / base / final / vacated / leaving / wilco / roger / going around), the specific rule above must win. Without this guard 'Bern Tower N172SP ready for departure' in TOWER_CONTACT was misclassified as a fresh initial call. 'contact tower' and 'when ready' guard against Voxtral dropping the word 'contact' in frequency-handoff readbacks.
Generic initial call — last resort if no specific facility variant matched. Mirrors the old match_initial_call() that delegated to ground OR tower.
Last-resort fallback for traffic-advisory replies. Single keyword 'looking' is ambiguous, so confidence is low and it appears at the very end of the rule list.

adjustments._note: Upgrade INITIAL_CALL_INBOUND → INITIAL_CALL_INBOUND_VRP when a VRP was named in the transcript (airport_vrps lookup populated msg.vrp_name).
Airborne intent while on ground — likely a readback of a clearance ('takeoff runway 06, report downwind'). Demote to READBACK at moderate confidence so the state machine treats it as such.
Ground intent while airborne — physically impossible. Demote confidence so the LM gets a chance to reclassify.
SELF_ANNOUNCE only makes sense on UNICOM at non-towered fields. At a towered airport the word 'traffic' is almost never a CTAF announcement.
Pilot called 'tower' at a non-towered airport — phraseology error. Lower confidence so the LM can correct the intent.
Post-landing 'request taxi' is a taxi-to-apron request, not a pre-departure clearance request. State machine has just seen LANDING_CLEARED (or TOUCH_AND_GO_CLEARED) within the just_landed window.

Defense in depth: an INITIAL_CALL_* intent only makes phraseological sense in the initial-contact window (IDLE / GROUND_CONTACT / TAXI_CLEARED — the latter to cover the tower_only auto-advance hop). Outside it (TOWER_CONTACT, PATTERN_ENTRY, LANDING_CLEARED, EN_ROUTE, ...) the matcher landed on the wrong rule; demote confidence so the LM can reclassify.
